title: Iwi affiliation (estimated counts): 2018 | Stats NZ
content:
## Iwi affiliation (estimated counts): 2018
22 June 2021, 10:45am
Iwi affiliation estimated counts present the number of people identified as being of Māori descent in the 2018 Census.
E ngā mana, e ngā reo, tēnei te reo mihi ki ngā pari kārangaranga o te motu, tēnā koutou katoa.
Iwi affiliation estimated counts are now available following a joint initiative with Data Iwi Leaders Group (Data ILG). For many purposes, the 2018 iwi estimates provide a more relevant and up-to-date picture than continued use of 2013 Census iwi data.
These iwi estimates are not official 2018 Census counts, and the quality of these estimates ranges from moderate to poor depending on how the data is used.
Strengths and limitations of 2018 iwi affiliation estimated counts provides guidance on how to use these estimates.
More information is available via:
- 2018 iwi affiliation estimated counts – DataInfo+ provides more detailed description of the quality of the data and the framework used to evaluate the data.
- Iwi affiliation (estimated counts): 2018 data sources and quality by iwi provides specific quality information for iwi.
The counts are now available on Te Whata the iwi data platform, as key indicators and charts, and NZ.Stat as customisable tables for download.
There are a total of 29 new NZ.Stat tables grouped by variables such as sex, age group, total personal income, and highest qualification.
On NZ.Stat , select Iwi estimates 2018 , or use:
- Iwi affiliation (estimated count), 2018
- Iwi grouping (estimated count), 2018
Download the estimated counts of iwi affiliation and grouping in Excel and CSV formats below.
- Iwi affiliation (estimated counts): 2018 Excel spreadsheet, 29 KB

### Iwi affiliation (estimated counts): 2018
Excel spreadsheet, 29 KB
- Iwi affiliation (estimated counts): 2018 – CSV ZIP compressed file, 3 KB

### Iwi affiliation (estimated counts): 2018 – CSV
ZIP compressed file, 3 KB
No further iwi estimates using other census variables is currently planned to be released on the Stats NZ website, but further data is available via:
- Te Ara Takatū – provides customised data for iwi
- Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) – approved users can access this large research database. Given the joint nature of this work and quality of the data, Data ILG will provide guidance to Stats NZ when assessing IDI applications requesting this dataset.
Stats NZ and Data ILG will look to produce a range of time series using this data within the IDI to support iwi. An example of this is the Job Seeker Support series on Te Whata .

## Background to the 2018 iwi affiliation counts
The 2018 iwi affiliation estimated counts provide estimates of iwi population counts and characteristics for people identified as being of Māori descent in the 2018 Census. They include, for the first time, information for 32 iwi added to Stats NZ’s iwi classification in 2017 .
In April 2019, Stats NZ announced official statistical counts of iwi from the 2018 Census would not be published due to missing iwi affiliation data and a lack of alternative government data sources to fill gaps in the data.
Under the Mana Ōrite Relationship Agreement, Stats NZ and technicians of the Data ILG worked together to explore options and develop appropriate methods to address the high level of missing data in the 2018 Census.  This work has resulted in usable estimated counts for iwi and iwi data users..
As a result, the 2018 iwi estimates are useful for providing:
- updated population counts for all iwi
- a more recent representation of iwi characteristics (for example, age and income) compared with the 2013 Census
- information for the 32 iwi who have been added to the classification since 2013.
The 2018 iwi estimates are not recommended for:
- comparing counts with previous census years
- measuring small populations (of less than 100 people).
The 2018 iwi estimated counts are based on:
- 2018 Census iwi responses for people identified as being of Māori descent
- iwi affiliation responses from 2013 Census
- iwi affiliation of parents for children under 15 years
- statistical methods to adjust for the remaining missing data.
The methods applied to the 2018 Census data were designed specifically for the high levels of missing data. It is not expected that these methods will be applied to census data in the future. For the 2023 Census, Stats NZ is developing several activities to help improve response rates for Māori.
Methodology for the 2018 iwi affiliation estimated counts provides a detailed description of the methodology used to produce these iwi estimates.
A significant work programme is underway to support improved response rates for Māori in the 2023 Census and deliver quality data for iwi-Māori. Programme initiatives include a larger community engagement team to work with Māori communities, employing more and local people to assist with the collection of data at census time, and a range of activities to support access to and understanding of census. Stats NZ will also pilot iwi-led collection as part of the 2023 Census.
For more information about 2023 Census, please contact info @stats.govt.nz
Sandi Reily 021 285 9191 media@stats.govt.nz
TeTohu@stats.govt.nz
